diagrams.title: Winner Tree for 4 Sorted Sequences
diagrams.description: Complete winner tree showing merging of 4 sorted sequences with initial elements [3], [1], [4], [2]. The tree stores winners (smaller values) at internal nodes.
elements: Winner path highlighted from leaf containing 1 to root
diagrams.purpose: Visualize complete winner tree structure and understand how minimum element is determined at root

diagrams.title: Loser Tree Update Operation
diagrams.description: Shows how loser tree update works when element at one leaf is replaced. Demonstrates that only comparison with stored loser is needed at each level.
elements: Initial tree with winner at root
Replace leaf value, show comparison with stored loser
Winner propagates upward by competing with stored losers
Path from leaf to root highlighted
diagrams.purpose: Understand update operation efficiency in loser trees compared to winner trees

diagrams.title: Non-Power-of-Two Handling with Dummy Leaves
diagrams.description: Shows how to handle 5 sequences by adding dummy leaves with +∞ value to make complete binary tree.
elements: Dummy leaves always lose in comparisons
Result: tree behaves as if 8 sequences with 3 empty
diagrams.purpose: Understand handling of non-power-of-two leaf counts in selection trees
